wave1_lesson_summary: All 8 wave1 functions scored T3=1.0 under both C2 and C3 advisory conditions, confirming that wave1 complexity is uniformly too easy for frontier models and wave2 must source harder candidates from outside the current raw/ pool.

hard_boundaries: Authorable T2 ground truth: bug injection must be tractable without formal verification
Pure or nearly-pure function: no heavy I/O, no class state, no external data dependencies

pass: Multi-step logic with non-obvious bug locations
Loop termination conditions involving multiple variables
blocked: Single-operator flip is the only injectable bug (e.g., > vs >= only)
exclude: Trivially obvious behavior from name alone

pass: Has semantic structure admitting a non-trivial behavioral transform
Output type/structure change alters downstream usage
Tie-breaking policy change with observable behavioral difference
Accumulation strategy inversion (e.g., inclusive vs exclusive semantics)
Boundary semantics change (e.g., open vs closed interval)
blocked: All valid transforms collapse into optional-parameter defaults
exclude: Only trivial constant substitution; no meaningful semantic transform exists

wave1_easy_family_exclusion.characterization: Short utility helpers (8-20 executable lines), all behavior obvious from name/docstring, single-loop or single-scan structure, T3 transforms trivially deducible from context
wave1_easy_family_exclusion.exclude_if: Similar complexity, structure, and T2/T3 profile to any of the 8 wave1 items

truth_tractability.pass: Bug location and expected behavior authoring-tractable without formal verification
truth_tractability.blocked: Requires complex oracle (e.g., NP-hard correctness check)
truth_tractability.exclude: Truth authoring requires external system or data

representation_value.pass: Richer type information or contracts plausibly help orient the model vs raw source
representation_value.weak: Any representation gives essentially equivalent context

rubric_table.PASS: One def, no self-call or mutual recursion
Non-trivial bug family tractable to author and verify
rubric_table.EXCLUDE: Requires external oracle or formal verification

rubric_table.PASS: Multi-step logic; non-obvious bug location; edge-case-rich
rubric_table.BLOCKED: Single operator flip only; too trivial

rubric_table.PASS: Admits non-trivial behavioral transform with real semantic content
Meaningfully harder than wave1 (complexity, structure, edge cases)
rubric_table.EXCLUDE: Same complexity profile as wave1 helpers

rubric_table.PASS: Author can specify and verify correct behavior without external tools
Type contracts or structured context plausibly help C3/C4 over C1

sources_examined.source: data/functions/raw/ — functions NOT in wave1 and NOT pilot anchors
sources_examined.note: raw/ contains exactly 11 .py files: 3 pilot anchors + 8 wave1 functions. Zero uncharacterized files remain.

sources_examined.source: candidates.json — entries at stage1_pass or pre-stage2 state
sources_examined.note: All non-anchor entries in candidates.json are wave1-authored with stage3_result=ceiling_risk. demo_func has final_decision=EXCLUDE (smoke-test only). No pre-stage2 entries exist.
data/dataset/ contains only candidates.json and functions_manifest.json. No intake queue, no stage1 CSV, no additional tracker files.

bounded_candidate_pool.conclusion: The bounded candidate pool is EMPTY. The raw/ directory is exhausted. All 11 function files are either wave1-authored or pilot anchors. No new candidates can be triaged from existing repo sources.

diversity_audit.balancing_note: No candidates to audit. Pool expansion required before diversity analysis is possible.

final_decision: WAVE2 SOURCING INSUFFICIENT — EXPAND CANDIDATE POOL BEFORE AUTHORING
next_action: Manually author and commit 15-25 new Python function source files to data/functions/raw/ that satisfy the wave2 sourcing rubric (harder difficulty families: stateful scan/segment logic with multiple interacting state variables, ordering/tie-policy logic, grouping/partitioning with non-trivial boundary conditions, accumulation with multi-step invariants, string/sequence processing with multi-step logic), then re-run Phase 4A triage.

target_difficulty_families: stateful scan / segment / window logic with multiple interacting state variables (harder than count_true_segments)
ordering / tie-policy / sort-variant logic (harder than first_index_of_max)
grouping / restructuring / partitioning logic (harder than batch_list)
accumulation / aggregation with multi-step invariants (harder than rle_encode)
two-pointer or dual-cursor merge/scan patterns (harder than merge_sorted)
pool_expansion_guidance.complexity_target: 20-40 executable lines, 2+ distinct loop structures or nested conditionals, non-obvious bug locations
avoid: Functions with only single-operator-flip injectable bugs
Functions where T3 transform is trivially a constant substitution
Functions with behavior fully obvious from name and docstring
